food_subgroup: Instant coffee and tea sachet gift set

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a packaged Singaporean kopi sachet and tea gift set. FOODON_03301710 (coffee beverage) is used as the closest parent for the coffee component. The tea component maps to FOODON_03301711 (tea beverage) but the composite gift-set product has no exact FoodOn entry.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest parent for the kopi sachet component. No FoodEx2 code exists for a composite kopi-and-tea retail gift set. The tea component maps to FoodEx2 A00LG (Tea and herbal infusions) but the combined product is not individually coded.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34658001 (Coffee substance) is used as the closest parent for the kopi component. No SNOMED CT concept exists for a packaged Singaporean kopi sachet and Straits tea retail gift set.

allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.description: 1 sachet of kopi (approx. 30 g powder dissolved in 180 ml hot water)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one kopi sachet (approximately 30 g of powder) dissolved in 180 ml of hot water, yielding a sweetened, creamy Nanyang-style coffee drink of roughly 210 ml.

nutrient_source.title: Instant 3-in-1 coffee mix, prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented by HPB Singapore nutrient data for kopi and Nanyang-style instant coffee
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for the Toast Box Signatures gift set as a composite retail product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Beverages, coffee, instant, with whitener and sugar' (FDC ID 171891) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore data for kopi (Nanyang-style instant coffee with creamer and sugar). Values are expressed per 100 g of prepared beverage (powder dissolved in water at standard dilution). The Straits Classic Tea component, when prepared as plain tea, contributes negligible macronutrients and is treated as a near-zero-calorie beverage for the per-100g basis. The composite per-100g values reflect the kopi sachet preparation as the dominant caloric component of the set.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of instant coffee with sugar and non-dairy creamer — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee with sugar (GI ~53) and Atkinson FS et al. 2021 peer-reviewed GI table
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Toast Box kopi sachets. GI of 53 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for instant coffee prepared with sugar and non-dairy creamer, which is the closest analogous product. The Straits Classic Tea component prepared as plain tea has a GI effectively of 1 (no glycemic carbohydrates). The composite GI of 53 reflects the kopi sachet as the dominant glycemic component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (53 × 7.2 × 0.30 kg serving factor adjusted to 30 g powder in 210 ml) ≈ 4.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content per serving; caffeine may cause transient blood pressure elevation
hypertension.guidance: Each kopi sachet prepared beverage contains approximately 5–6 mg sodium per 100 ml, posing minimal sodium load for hypertensive individuals; however, caffeine in Robusta-based kopi may transiently raise blood pressure and individuals with poorly controlled hypertension should limit intake to one to two cups per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content from pre-sweetened kopi sachet formulation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each standard kopi sachet contains approximately 6–8 g of added sugar per serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that regular consumption of pre-sweetened instant kopi sachets contributes to daily free sugar intake and may impair glycaemic control, and should consider requesting unsweetened kopi-O kosong sachets where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from non-dairy creamer; moderate caffeine intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The non-dairy creamer in kopi sachets contributes a small amount of saturated fat (approximately 0.2 g per serving) and may contain partially hydrogenated vegetable oils in some formulations; individuals with cardiovascular disease should check the specific sachet ingredient label and limit consumption if trans fats are present, while moderate coffee consumption (up to three cups daily) is not contraindicated in stable cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Toast Box Signatures is a retail gift set sold by Toast Box, a Singaporean kopitiam chain, comprising one box of kopi (Nanyang-style instant coffee) sachets of the customer's choice and one box of Straits Classic Tea sachets. Kopi is a traditional Singaporean and Malaysian coffee beverage brewed from Robusta beans roasted with sugar and butter or margarine, then blended with sweetened condensed milk or non-dairy creamer to produce a rich, sweet, and slightly bitter cup. The Straits Classic Tea is a blend of black tea leaves inspired by the Peranakan and colonial-era tea culture of the Straits Settlements, offering a smooth, mildly astringent flavour. Both products are in instant sachet format requiring only the addition of hot water. Nutritionally, the kopi sachet preparation provides moderate calories (approximately 38 kcal per 100 ml prepared), primarily from added sugar and non-dairy creamer, with low protein and fat content. The Straits tea, when prepared without added sugar, is essentially calorie-free. The set is commonly purchased as a gift or souvenir representing Singapore's kopitiam heritage. Clinically, the pre-sweetened kopi sachets are relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to added sugar content, and the non-dairy creamer component may be of concern for those monitoring saturated fat intake for cardiovascular health. The caffeine content from Robusta coffee is higher than Arabica and may be relevant for individuals with hypertension or caffeine sensitivity.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Toast Box kopi sachet?
What is in the Toast Box gift set?
How do I prepare Toast Box kopi sachet?
Is Toast Box kopi suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in Toast Box kopi?

confidence_reason: No exact nutrient entry exists in HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central for the Toast Box Signatures gift set as a composite retail product. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest analogous USDA entry for instant 3-in-1 coffee (FDC ID 171891) and cross-referenced with HPB Singapore kopi data. The GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened instant coffee. Confidence is medium because the adaptation is defensible and well-documented but not an exact match.
notes: 1. Per-100g values are expressed on a prepared-beverage basis (kopi sachet dissolved in hot water at standard dilution), as this is the most nutritionally meaningful basis for a beverage product. The Straits Classic Tea component prepared as plain tea contributes negligible macronutrients. 2. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0; this is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for the analogous instant coffee with creamer entry, though individual Toast Box sachet formulations should be verified against the product label as some non-dairy creamers may contain trace trans fats. 3. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for prepared instant coffee beverages — naturally zero as a liquid beverage with no fibre-contributing solids. 4. cholesterol_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for non-dairy creamer-based instant coffee — naturally zero as no animal-derived cholesterol-containing ingredients are present in standard formulations. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; naturally zero as coffee and tea beverages do not contain ascorbic acid. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central entry for this food category. 7. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 8. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 9. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 10. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 11. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 12. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 13. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 14. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 15. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this product type. 16. glycemic_index is set to 53 as an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened instant coffee with non-dairy creamer; gi_category is low. 17. The food_name has been expanded from the field survey menu label 'Toast Box Signatures' to the more descriptive canonical form 'Toast Box Kopi Sachet and Straits Classic Tea Gift Set' to satisfy Q1 canonical identity requirements; 'Toast Box Signatures' is retained as the primary English alias. 18. Halal status is based on Toast Box's publicly stated halal certification by MUIS (Islamic Religious Council of Singapore); this should be verified against current certification status.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Expanded food_name from field survey menu label to canonical descriptive English name; added all five mandatory Singaporean language local_names; populated full per_100g nutrient profile from USDA FDC ID 171891 adapted for kopi preparation; set GI to 53 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database); added all three health_context conditions with clinical guidance; wrote 1,247-character LLM description; populated 21-entry aliases list with full aliases_by_language consistency; documented all -1 sentinel values and all zero values in notes; set ontology codes with closest_parent match_type and gap notes.

Iteration 2: Addressed Q8 failure from previous validation. The romanized alias 'Tou Si Gong Fang Ka Fei Bao Ji Hai Xia Jing Dian Cha Li He Tao Zhuang' was missing from the flat 'aliases' list despite being present in 'aliases_by_language'. This entry has been added to the flat 'aliases' list to ensure perfect consistency between the two fields, resolving the Q8 failure. All -1 fields documented in notes. Zero-macro justification phrases present. Ontology match_type values use only allowed strings. Confirmed GI > 0. Final self-score: 8/8, all hard gates pass — PASS.
